exercises.title: SAFE INDEX
exercises.brief: Read a key, or fall back.
promptLines: Define **getOr(r: Record<string, number>, k: string, d: number): number** returning `r[k]` when the key is present, otherwise `d`.
starterLines: function getOr(r: Record<string, number>, k: string, d: number): number {
solutionLines: function getOr(r: Record<string, number>, k: string, d: number): number {
return k in r ? r[k] : d;

exercises.title: PEAK SIGNAL
exercises.brief: Find the key holding the biggest value.
promptLines: Define **maxKey(r: Record<string, number>): string** returning the key whose value is **highest**.
On an **empty** record return `''`. On a tie, return the **first** key seen with that max.
exercises.hint: Track the best key so far; replace it only when r[k] is strictly greater.
exercises.lore: The loudest node on the grid wins the trace.

exercises.title: LIVE COUNT
exercises.brief: Count how many flags are raised.
promptLines: Define **countTrue(r: Record<string, boolean>): number** returning **how many values are `true`**.
countTrue({ a: true, b: false, c: true }) // 2

tests.name: two true
tests.code: assertEqual(countTrue({ a: true, b: false, c: true }), 2);

exercises.hint: Loop the keys and add 1 for every truthy value.
exercises.lore: How many sentries are still awake on the wire?

exercises.title: MIRROR MAP
exercises.brief: Swap every key with its value.
promptLines: Define **invert(r: Record<string, string>): Record<string, string>** returning a **new** record where each value becomes a key and each key becomes its value.
starterLines: function invert(r: Record<string, string>): Record<string, string> {
solutionLines: function invert(r: Record<string, string>): Record<string, string> {
exercises.hint: const out = {}; for (const k in r) out[r[k]] = k; return out;
exercises.lore: Flip the lookup and read the grid in reverse.

exercises.title: BUCKET PUSH
exercises.brief: Add to a tally without touching the original.
promptLines: Define **addToBucket(r: Record<string, number>, k: string, n: number): Record<string, number>** returning a **new** record where `r[k]` is increased by `n`. A missing key starts at **0**. Do **not** mutate the input.
starterLines: function addToBucket(r: Record<string, number>, k: string, n: number): Record<string, number> {
solutionLines: function addToBucket(r: Record<string, number>, k: string, n: number): Record<string, number> {

tests.name: input not mutated

exercises.hint: Copy r into a new object first, then set out[k] = (k in out ? out[k] : 0) + n.
exercises.lore: Never write back to the shared ledger — fork it.
